title: rubus erythroxanthus var. immerens
intro: Rubus erythroxanthus var. immerens, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a variant of the Rubus erythroxanthus plant species, which belongs to the rose family and is native to the Himalayan region. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Himalayan raspberry has been reported to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating gastrointestinal issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed for their medicinal properties.
